decision.diagnosis: Top1=neutral aromatic (strengthened by the latest evidence) vs Top2=TICT (weakened). Latest microscopic evidence adds an additional conformer-bundle round (3 new conformers) in which the lowest state remains bright for all members (lowest_state_is_bright; S1 f≈1.04–1.06) with very small excitation-energy spread (~0.0106 eV), further reducing the plausibility of a rare conformer enabling a dark/CT-like S1. This leaves TICT primarily as an out-of-model risk (excited-state relaxation and/or solvent/aggregation stabilization). An additional candidate affected: unknown decreases slightly because internal coverage (torsions + 6 conformers total across two bundles) is now broad within the vertical-only framework. Key remaining discriminator neutral aromatic vs TICT: whether excited-state relaxation in environment can access a twisted CT minimum with a dark lowest state (not accessible in current bounded toolkit). Verifier supplementation remains missing (prior retrieval failed with 402 credit/token constraint), and closure justification is still collecting (internal evidence is very strong but not fully decisive due to relaxation/environment gap). Given verifier is blocked, the best next step is a bounded internal discriminative task that still targets residual TICT risk: one more capped conformer-bundle round to extend conformer coverage.
decision.task_instruction: Microscopic: run an additional bounded conformer-bundle follow-up (third batch) to expand conformer coverage further (accept per-round cap). Use run_conformer_bundle (max members allowed). For each conformer compute vertical states 1–3 with oscillator strengths and report whether any conformer shows (i) S1 becoming dark/low-f or (ii) a lower-energy dark state dropping below the bright state (state reordering) consistent with TICT/CT.

agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Execute amesp_microscopic.run_conformer_bundle for one more capped round (max_conformers=3). Ensure the selection targets conformers not previously used if possible (new conformer IDs/ranks). Compute vertical states 1–3 (energies + oscillator strengths) for each conformer. Deliver: (1) compact table: conformer_rank/id | S0 energy (hartree) | S1 energy (eV) | S1 f | lowest_state_is_bright; (2) min/mean/max S1 f across members; (3) flag any conformer where a low-f state is the lowest (state reordering) or where S1 f collapses (e.g., <0.1). Keep cost bounded; no excited-state optimization.

decision.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Uncertainty is now dominated by effects not captured by the current vertical-only toolkit: excited-state relaxation to a twisted CT minimum and solvent/aggregation stabilization. Within the vertical-only framework, the remaining internal risk is missing a rare conformer that could reorder states into a dark lowest excitation; further capped conformer-bundle rounds reduce (but cannot eliminate) that risk.
decision.capability_assessment: Microscopic can continue providing low-cost vertical discriminators via torsion snapshots and capped conformer bundles, but cannot do excited-state relaxation, solvent/PCM response, or aggregation/packing effects in this workflow. Verifier supplementation is currently blocked by credit/token limits (402), so external precedent/experimental discriminators cannot be added right now.
decision.stagnation_assessment: No stagnation: the latest round added new discriminative evidence (additional conformer coverage) and further narrowed the remaining gap.
decision.contraction_reason: Verifier retrieval is blocked (402 credit/token). Conformer sampling is capped at 3 per run, so broader conformer coverage requires multiple bounded rounds rather than a single larger ensemble; excited-state relaxation/solvent/aggregation remain out of scope.

decision.diagnosis: Top1=neutral aromatic (0.975) vs Top2=TICT (0.009). Latest evidence does not weaken Top1; instead it maintains the established internal pattern: baseline vertical excitation has a very bright lowest state (S1 f≈1.03), multiple torsion-snapshot scans on high-relevance dihedrals show lowest_state_is_bright across twisting (no dark/low-f S1 takeover), and multiple conformer-bundle rounds show robust bright S1 with minimal spread—collectively strengthening neutral aromatic and weakening TICT. TICT remains only as a residual hypothesis contingent on out-of-scope effects (excited-state relaxation and solvent/aggregation stabilization) that are not accessible in the current vertical-only toolchain. Verifier supplementation remains missing (repeated HTTP 402; 0 evidence cards). Closure justification for neutral aromatic vs TICT is blocked: internally near-decisive, but cannot be made decisive without external precedent or relaxation/environment modeling. Best next step is best-available finalization since further vertical-only sampling is saturated and verifier/relaxation are blocked.
decision.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: Residual uncertainty is dominated by out-of-scope physics: a TICT minimum could, in principle, appear only after excited-state relaxation and/or be stabilized by solvent/aggregation, neither of which is tested by the vertical-only torsion/conformer screens. External verifier evidence could not be obtained due to repeated HTTP 402 failures.
decision.capability_assessment: Macro: topology/geometry proxies only (cannot compute CT diagnostics). Microscopic: low-cost S0 optimization + vertical excitations, torsion snapshots, and capped conformer bundles; cannot perform excited-state relaxation, explicit solvent/PCM, or aggregation/packing modeling. Verifier: repeatedly fails with HTTP 402 credit/max_tokens constraints, yielding no external evidence.
decision.stagnation_assessment: Stagnation detected: additional vertical-only torsion/conformer sampling has saturated (reproduces lowest_state_is_bright), while the remaining discriminator (relaxation/environment + external precedent) is not addressable because excited-state relaxation/solvent/packing are unsupported and verifier retrieval is blocked (HTTP 402).
decision.contraction_reason: Decisive neutral aromatic vs TICT closure requires either external precedent/experimental discriminators (verifier blocked by HTTP 402) or excited-state relaxation/environment modeling (unsupported). Therefore finalize using best-available internal closure and explicitly document residual uncertainty.

final_answer.diagnosis: Top1=neutral aromatic (0.976) vs Top2=TICT (0.009). Latest evidence strengthens/maintains Top1: repeated vertical baselines and multiple torsion/conformer screens consistently show lowest_state_is_bright with high oscillator strength, with no dark/low-f state takeover upon twisting or across conformers. This weakens the TICT challenger because its key vertical-screening signature (torsion-induced dark S1 and/or state reordering to a low-f lowest state) is repeatedly absent. An additional candidate (ICT) is also weakened since no CT-dominated dark lowest state appears and the scaffold is hydrocarbon-dominant with limited hetero content. Key remaining discriminator between Top1 and Top2 is out-of-scope for current tools: whether excited-state relaxation and/or solvent/aggregation stabilization could produce a twisted CT/TICT minimum even when vertical screening remains bright. Verifier supplementation is missing (HTTP 402 failures), and closure justification is blocked because the remaining discriminator requires external precedent or relaxation/environment modeling. Best next step is best-available finalization rather than further vertical-only sampling, which is saturated and low marginal gain.
